perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party or by another Haldex Group entity to the Supplier including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement;
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement. Confidential Information specifically excludes financial data.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' broadly includes all technical, financial, and business information. The modified definition adds a clause excluding 'financial data'. This introduces an in-text contradiction, especially given later clauses rely on a broad definition of confidential information, which is financial information. This alteration creates uncertainty regarding the protection of financial data shared between parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' to include all technical, financial and business information. The changed text adds an exclusion for 'financial data'. The contradicted text states the parties must treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret. Because the changed text specifically excludes financial data from the definition of confidential information, it contradicts the later clause mandating that all confidential information (which the original text would've included financial information, but not the changed text) be kept secret. This contradiction is clear and creates ambiguity about the handling of financial data, a strong contradiction based on definition 1.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as secret, private and confidential, unless otherwise required by law; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party, except legal counsel; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates treating Confidential Information as 'absolutely secret, private, and confidential.' The modified version adds exceptions: 'unless otherwise required by law' and 'except legal counsel.' This introduces an in-text contradiction because 'absolutely secret' is now conditional. Specifically clause v) is contradictied as it limits the disclosure only to directors, employees and consultants but now legal counsel is also being added. This weakens the obligation of confidentiality and allows for potentially broader disclosures than initially intended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as secret, private and confidential, unless otherwise required by law; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party, except legal counsel; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 mandates treating Confidential Information as 'absolutely secret, private, and confidential'. The modified text changes this to allow exceptions ('unless otherwise required by law' and 'except legal counsel'). The contradicted text is Section 3, which contains these exceptions. Clause v) also contains contradictions as it allows disclosure to only directors, employees, and consultants but the changed text now includes legal counsel, triggering an in-text contradiction. The added exceptions in the changed text directly contradict the idea of 'absolutely secret,' creating a conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Indemnification The Parties undertake to reimburse, indemnify and hold the other Party harmless from any damages, loss or expense incurred by it as a result of either Party violating the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Indemnification The Parties undertake to reimburse, indemnify and hold the other Party harmless from any damages, loss or expense incurred by it as a result of either Party negligently violating the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original indemnification clause applies to any violation of the agreement's terms. The modified clause limits indemnification to violations resulting from 'negligently' violating the agreement. This change requires proving negligence to trigger indemnification, making it more difficult to enforce the clause and contradicts the initial strict liability standard implied by the original agreement, as it shifts it to 'negligently'. This now creates ambiguity on the standard of breaching the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as secret, private and confidential, unless otherwise required by law; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party, except legal counsel; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original indemnification clause in Section 5 applied to any violation of the agreement's terms, implying strict liability. The changed clause limits indemnification to violations resulting from 'negligently' violating the agreement. This introduces a negligence standard for triggering indemnification. The contradicted text in Section 3 outlines specific undertakings regarding confidential information, such as preventing disclosure and ensuring compliance. Violating these undertakings could now be argued as not necessarily 'negligent,' thus potentially preventing indemnification under the modified Section 5, creating a contradiction in how breaches of the agreement, specifically Section 3, are handled and indemnified.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, may be settled first through mediation. If mediation fails, the dispute shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of one arbitrator. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates arbitration for all disputes. The modified clause introduces optional mediation before arbitration and changes the number of arbitrators from three to one. This contradicts the original, strictly binding arbitration agreement by providing an alternative dispute resolution method and reducing the arbitral panel. The introduction of mediation, before arbitration, means its not 'Any and all disputes' that must be solved by the Arbitration, but those disputes that were not solved by mediation first.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, may be settled first through mediation. If mediation fails, the dispute shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of one arbitrator. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 7.1 by introducing optional mediation before arbitration and alters the number of arbitrators from three to one. The original clause stated 'any and all disputes...shall be settled by arbitration'. By introducing mediation, the changed text contradicts the mandatory arbitration stated in the original text. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The original text contradicts the new changes. The contradiction is due to the change from mandatory arbitration to optional mediation followed by arbitration and the change in the number of arbitrators. This change causes ambiguity and inconsistency regarding dispute resolution methods and procedures, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive the termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years, unless terminated earlier as per section 8.4. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive for one (1) year after the termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement has an initial term of three years with automatic extensions, and confidentiality obligations survive termination indefinitely. The modified clause introduces a possibility of earlier termination and limits the survival of confidentiality obligations to one year after termination. This directly contradicts the initially stated indefinite survival of confidentiality, as its now limited to one year after the termination of the agreement. There is a contradiction also, since even if its being termined, the original text doesn't consider any early termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years, unless terminated earlier as per section 8.4. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive for one (1) year after the termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 8.1 by adding 'unless terminated earlier as per section 8.4' to the initial term clause and limiting the survival of confidentiality obligations to 'one (1) year after the termination of this Agreement'. This directly contradicts the implied indefinite survival of confidentiality obligations stated earlier. While both the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the change to the original text causes this contradiction to appear. The original text stated an indefinite confidentiality, while the changed text limits it to one year, which creates the contradiction within the document itself, making enforcement of either term unclear.

perturbation.original_text: DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 Haldex Non -disclosure Agreement May 2015. REG NO: HDX4- 51-130 This non-disclosure agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into by and between ______________ _____ ____, with company registration number _______ ____________, address at ___________________________ _______ and fax number _____________ ___ hereinafter referred to as “Haldex”, and ____________ __________, with company registration numbe r ___________________ , address at _______ ___________________________ and fax number_______ ______________ hereinafter referred to as the Supplier. Haldex and Supplier are hereinafter jointly referred to as Parties and individually as Party.
perturbation.changed_text: DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 Haldex Non -disclosure Agreement May 2015. REG NO: HDX4- 51-130 This non-disclosure agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into by and between ______________ _____ ____, with company registration number _______ ____________, address at ___________________________ _______ and fax number _____________ ___ hereinafter referred to as “Haldex”, and ____________ __________, with company registration numbe r ___________________ , address at _______ ___________________________ and fax number_______ ______________ hereinafter referred to as the Supplier. Haldex and Supplier are hereinafter jointly referred to as Parties and individually as Haldex.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines Haldex and the Supplier as individual Parties. The modified text states that Haldex and Supplier are jointly referred to as Parties, and individually as Haldex. This creates a contradiction, as it implies the Supplier is also referred to as 'Haldex' individually, which is incorrect and nonsensical. This undermines the fundamental definition of the involved parties and can cause confusion throughout the document when referencing either company. It now creates ambiguity as there can now be confusion if the agreement means Haldex, or the Supplier, by writing Haldex.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Confidential Information shall mean: i) all technical, financial and business information disclosed by one Party to the other Party including, but not limited to, documents, data, information, prototypes or components relating to devices, methods, materials, apparatus, d esigns, research, yields and specifications; and ii) information relating to the existence of this Agreement. Confidential Information specifically excludes financial data.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Parties'. Originally, 'Haldex' and 'Supplier' are individually referred to as 'Party'. The changed text redefines this, stating that 'Haldex' and 'Supplier' are individually referred to as 'Haldex'. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which states that confidential information is disclosed by one 'Party' to the other 'Party'. If the 'Supplier' is now also referred to as 'Haldex' individually, it creates ambiguity and directly conflicts with the original meaning of 'Party' within the document, creating uncertainty about which entity is disclosing or receiving confidential information, undermining the definition of the involved parties and creating ambiguity in the agreement, and making it a strong in-text contradiction.
